matchup: Oklahoma City Thunder at San Antonio Spurs
venue: Frost Bank Center, San Antonio, TX

b003_premarket_stress_test.projection_timestamp_context: pre-market Game 3 forecast before final market scan

okc_moneyline.diagnosis: Correct directional read. OKC was the cleaner side and won by margin.

okc_team_total.diagnosis: Strongest game-level scoring hit. OKC cleared by 9.2 points over model mean and 9.5 over fair line.

sas_team_total.diagnosis: Pregame caution was correct. SAS landed almost exactly on the modeled median and below the live 110.5 strike line.

full_game_total.diagnosis: Over direction was valid, but only with playable pregame entry at 219.5 or lower. Live market inflation reduced edge quality.

spread_margin.diagnosis: Correct side but under-projected separation by 10.3 points.

scenario_tree_review.closest_realized_path: Scenario B/C hybrid despite Fox active
scenario_tree_review.diagnosis: Fox played and Harper played, but SAS still behaved like a creation-fragile team once OKC pressure settled in. The model over-credited Fox as a stabilizer and underweighted OKC guard-depth pressure plus bench shooting ceiling.
scenario_tree_review.scenario_correction: Fox active should not automatically stabilize SAS if OKC pressure, live-ball turnovers, and bench spacing are simultaneously elevated.

sga_points.note: Usage converted more through assists and FT stabilization than raw scoring.

sga_assists.note: Jalen Williams absence boost to facilitation was validated.

wembanyama_rebounds.note: Largest model failure. Rebound tree collapsed due to OKC efficiency, spacing, Wembanyama contest load, and FT-heavy rhythm disruption.

hartenstein_rebounds.note: Foul/minute drag and rebound redistribution reduced ceiling.

chet_rebounds.note: Frontcourt rebound volume was lower than expected while OKC perimeter/bench rebounding absorbed chances.

castle_points.note: FT volume helped but field-goal efficiency and foul pressure limited ceiling.

jared_mccain_points.note: Jalen Williams absence boost was directionally right, but model underweighted McCain bench scoring ceiling.

primary_model_leak.leak_name: Elite big rebound tree overconfidence under opponent efficiency spike
primary_model_leak.description: The model projected Wembanyama and OKC frontcourt rebounds from prior chance volume without sufficiently suppressing rebound opportunity when opponent 3P efficiency, FT rate, and spacing/contest load all spiked.
failed_assumptions: Wembanyama minutes and prior rebound chance dominance would carry forward directly.
Fox active would stabilize SAS offense enough to preserve competitive rebound flow.
OKC frontcourt rebounds would remain concentrated through Hartenstein/Chet instead of spreading to guards/wings/bench.

b003_premarket_stress_test.stress_test_summary: B.003 correctly identified OKC as the clean side, OKC team total strength, and SAS team total fragility. The main failure was rebound modeling, especially Wembanyama, where prior chance dominance was not sufficiently suppressed by OKC efficiency, spacing, foul environment, and defensive contest load.

model_snapshots.game_state_read: SAS needed 27 points in 11:06 while trailing by 13 with inefficient halfcourt offense and foul pressure on Castle/Keldon/Wembanyama.

okc.shai_gilgeous_alexander: 26 PTS, 12 AST, 12/12 FT; late-game stabilization through free throws and table-setting.
okc.jared_mccain: 24 PTS off bench; major spacing and shot-creation swing.
okc.jaylin_williams: 18 PTS, 5/6 3PT; key bench variance spike that pushed OKC scoring above baseline.
okc.hartenstein: 8 rebounds, 5 fouls; relevant to rebound redistribution and late frontcourt foul risk.

sas.wembanyama: 26 PTS but only 4 REB; scoring remained alive, rebounding muted.
sas.castle: 14 PTS, 7 AST, 5 PF; whistle volume helped but foul pressure reduced clean late-game ceiling.
sas.fox: 15 PTS, 7 REB, 6 AST; active but not enough downhill FT creation.
sas.keldon_johnson: 5 fouls in 11:40; reduced Spurs physical pressure and rotation flexibility.

phase_analysis.q1: SAS jumped to a 15-point lead behind Fox pace, Wembanyama early shot-making, and OKC turnovers. OKC bench threes reduced damage and kept game within 5 by quarter end.
phase_analysis.q2: Game flipped. OKC won 32-20 behind 63.2% shooting and 71.4% from three. SAS shot 31.2% and committed five turnovers for 12 OKC points.
phase_analysis.q3: Whistle-heavy quarter. OKC scored 37, SAS scored 33, but OKC maintained separation. SAS needed free throws to stay alive while OKC kept hitting enough perimeter shots.
phase_analysis.q4: OKC closed 28-24. SAS reached 108, falling short of the 110.5 live team total. Late bench points moved OKC to 123 and full-game total to 231.

positions: SAS over 110.5 TT NO avg -133: WIN

successful_reads: Halftime SAS team total under edge was real and survived fourth-quarter foul risk.
OKC team total over 108.5 pre/live position was correctly identified as structurally strong.
SAS offensive output depended too heavily on whistle and difficult halfcourt looks.
OKC bench shooting depth created ceiling pressure on OKC scoring lanes.
miss_or_caution_zones: Full-game under would have been fragile due to OKC bench and late reserve scoring.
SAS under survived but only by 2.5 points; late garbage-time threes remained a real tail risk.
Need better late-game reserve-scoring volatility detection when favorite lead is 14+ inside final two minutes.
